meta.note: v9.5.14 (2026-07-11): populated financial_engine.pricing_archetype on all 70 real services_specified (was 0/70). Empirically tested the 5 formula/assembly_formula-typed services_specified directly against computeArchetypeQuote before classifying any of them -- confirmed furniture_assembly_flat_pack/furniture_disassembly_for_moving/furniture_repair_hourly give exactly $0 under 'formula'/'tiered_per_unit' (the exact regression a prior, separate session's history describes finding and fixing) and must stay hourly_timed; cabinet_knob_or_pull_install and pax_wardrobe_assembly compute real, correct, non-zero results and are safely tiered_per_unit/formula respectively. All other services_specified classified by direct, existing fields (checkout_state=diagnostic -> diagnostic_open; type=hourly -> hourly_timed; type=flat_rate -> flat_simple). Confirmed the existing dispatch guard in computeUnifiedQuote (only overrides price for tiered_per_unit/formula) was already correct before touching any data -- this is purely a data change, zero code changes needed. v9.5.10 (2026-07-11): Phase 4 (Confidence Model Reconciliation) addressed -- raised minimum_quote_confidence from 40 to 65 for the 16 real specialized-tier services_specified that were still at the tier's original, lowest-in-the-system value (confirmed the contradiction was real and substantive: 15/16 give firm, non-diagnostic quotes at baseline). Judgment-based, evidence-informed, flagged for business-owner review -- see each service's own confidence_strategy._note for the full reasoning. v9.5.6 addendum: extended remote_deep_dive_modules (attempted_fix) to the 5 named services_specified newly reachable via checkout_state_override (washer_repair, microwave_repair, repair_appliances, toilet_flapper_or_fill_valve_replacement, furniture_repair_hourly) -- completes the loop so the Fork-in-the-Road mechanism activates for them too, not just dishwasher_repair. v9.5.6 (2026-07-10): First real content for checkout_state_override -- 5 existing client_response entries across symptom/toilet_symptom/issue marked diagnostic (Won't drain, Leaking water, 'Not sure -- just want it checked', Broken piece), plus 3 new symptom options added (Burning smell, Works intermittently, Trips breaker immediately), all using the correct top-level field shape (effects.{checkout_state_override} is deprecated/retired, not the live shape). Real, verified reach: washer_repair, microwave_repair, repair_appliances, toilet_flapper_or_fill_valve_replacement, and furniture_repair_hourly are all normally-quotable (standard_flat_rate) named services_specified that share these exact modules -- this is not inert data. See CHANGELOG for the full mapping from the original guidance table (which referenced symptom labels that, on direct check, don't exist verbatim in the real data) onto what's actually there. v9.5.4 (2026-07-10): Authored real remote_deep_dive_modules for all 24 'Diagnostic' service_archetypes entries (previously only dishwasher_repair had any), using only existing, real intake_modules -- attempted_fix universally, plus one category-appropriate second module where a real, non-redundant fit exists (appliance_type for the two generic appliance catch-alls, floor_type for floors_trim, standing_water for four fixture-proximate plumbing entries, device_type for computer_repair, internet_active for networking/smart_home). Deliberately did not force a second module onto entries without a genuinely confident fit (cabinets_drawers, doors, furniture, walls, windows, water_lines, cable_management) rather than pad the list -- see CHANGELOG for the full per-entry reasoning and the ones flagged for a domain sanity-check. Also corrected a stale '16 of 69 named services_specified' code comment (verified real count: 1 named + 24 dynamic = 25) in computeUnifiedQuote. v9.4 (2026-07-04): Major restructuring: all fee/minute surcharges moved to global_rules.modifiers; intake_modules now use modifier_ref; financial_engine simplified to {type, base_price, checkout_state, formula_ref?}; removed meta.global_rates and pricing_group_archetypes; stripped effects from smart_tags. schema_version bumped to 2.0. v9.3 (2026-06-21): meta.version and meta.schema_version are now tracked separately. version is the content/release counter (bumps on any meaningful edit: new services_specified, retagged smart_tags, new modules, etc). schema_version is the STRUCTURAL compatibility counter — it only bumps when the shape of the JSON changes in a way that would make old code misread the file (renamed/removed keys, new required fields, changed nesting). schema_version 1.0 represents the v9.1-and-later shape (intake_modules consolidation + params field on intake_chain steps was the last breaking structural change). v9.2 was pure qr.html engine fixes with no JSON shape change. v9.3 added checkout_state_override to the shared effects definition — additive and optional, so backward-compatible with schema_version 1.0 (old code ignoring an unrecognized field still reads the file correctly) — does not bump schema_version. See CHANGELOG_v9.1.md / v9.2.md / v9.3.md for full per-version detail; this note only tracks the version/schema_version split itself.

workflow._note: Phase 0 (Orchestrator Overhaul). This node is the active rulebook for routing, intake-question selection, confidence gating, and pricing -- not passive documentation. Every entry path (Catalog browse, Other-tile tap, free-text SmartQuote) must produce a BookingContext and pass it through executeWorkflow(context, db), which walks these steps in order. See Phase 5.5's _archaeology_notes on individual rules below for the specific real bug each rule exists to prevent -- these were extracted from actual hardcoded logic found and fixed during the architecture-audit sessions, not invented speculatively.

steps._note: Resolves BookingContext -> a concrete service or dynamic_service entity. For entry='catalog' or 'other_tile', selectedServiceId/selectedGroupId already pin this down directly. For entry='free_text', this step delegates to intent_mappings.objects keyword/synonym matching (NOT a hardcoded keyword map -- see the architecture-audit finding that resolveGroupFromIntent's hardcoded rules and intent_mappings.objects were two parallel, partially-overlapping systems; this workflow makes intent_mappings.objects the single real source going forward).

fallback._warn: no entity resolved with sufficient confidence

steps._note: Computes a small set of NAMED, independently-auditable booleans -- NOT a single opaque chainIsQtyOnly flag. Archaeology finding: the legacy chainIsQtyOnly check was too coarse to correctly handle behavior.bypass_intake services_specified, 3 of which (high_ceiling_bulb_replacement, shower_head_replacement, tub_spout_replacement) have ONE real, price-affecting, non-quantity question that must still be asked even though the service is otherwise flagged for minimal friction. A single boolean cannot represent that distinction; named, separate flags can.

produces.chain_is_pure_quantity: true only if every module in the resolved intake_chain is a recognized quantity-style module (item_count, count, hybrid_qty, global_quantity)
produces.chain_is_single_simple_question: true if the chain has exactly one module AND that module is item_count_template (a banded quantity question) -- distinct from chain_is_pure_quantity, since item_count_template is NOT in the legacy quantity-module set but is still simple enough to bypass extensive intake when paired with behavior.bypass_intake
produces.chain_has_real_non_quantity_question: true if the chain contains any module that is neither a quantity-style module nor item_count_template -- when true, intake bypass rules MUST still surface that question; bypassing it would silently drop a real, price-affecting answer
produces.all_visible_real_questions_answered: true once every currently-visible (per _isModVisible) non-quantity module has a real answer in BookingContext.answers

steps._note: Builds the final, real list of intake questions to ask: entity.intake_chain (the named modules) + global_rules.force_modules_by_variability[entity.confidence_strategy._variability_tier] (universal logistics/height questions, injected only when not already present) + then-branch targets unlocked by already-given answers. Archaeology finding: an earlier version of this logic (S._forceModules) was SET but never CONSUMED in the legacy curated-intake renderer -- confirmed and fixed mid-session; this step makes that consumption a mandatory, auditable part of the workflow rather than something that can silently regress again.

Archaeology finding (direct feedback during the session): the location chip/NLP-extracted location was being treated as cosmetic filler/notes despite carrying real, derivable intent (a 'front door' implies solid-core, a 'bedroom door' implies hollow-core; a 'toilet' near 'guest bedroom' is genuinely ambiguous and must NOT be guessed). This step pre-fills an intake answer ONLY when context.extractedLocation matches exactly one client_response[*].location_hints entry for a given module -- never when zero or multiple answers claim the same location, and never overriding an answer the user already gave.

Archaeology finding: the legacy curated-intake renderer used a hardcoded tagBonus (+5 skilled / +10 specialized, ADDED TO THE SCORE) instead of global_rules.confidence_escalation's real +15/+30 deltas (which RAISE THE BAR). These are mechanically different operations that were both firing in the same wrong direction -- confirmed via direct trace to make the Estimate button enable roughly 20 points too early for a real service (flatscreen_mounting_standard + #brick_wall: bar should rise 50->65, legacy code left it at 50 while also inflating the score). This step performs ONLY the bar-raising operation (confidence_escalation), and a separate, additive scoring step (base_confidence + module gains + NLP match confidence) computes the score independently -- the two must never be conflated into one number again.
See ui_template_matrix below -- this is the corrected version of Phase 3's example, using the named flags from compute_variability_flags instead of a single chainIsQtyOnly boolean.

Archaeology finding (post-session audit): 51 services_specified have real services_specified.*.required_materials and 35 have optional_materials, referencing real materials_catalog SKUs with real prices and a real markup_percent (confirmed uniformly 20% in global_rules.surcharges) -- NONE of which is read at runtime; every materials display reads the separate, often-stale default_estimates.materials.min/max instead (confirmed 3 real services_specified, e.g. dishwasher_repair, where the legacy number and the catalog-derived number would genuinely differ). Resolution, NOT deletion (Chesterton's Fence: default_estimates.materials.note frequently carries real, business-meaningful customer-facing explanation -- e.g. dishwasher_repair's 'Exact part cost cannot be determined before diagnosis' -- that has no equivalent in the catalog data and must be preserved): when required_materials/optional_materials is non-empty, compute min = sum(required SKU prices * (1+markup)), max = min + sum(optional SKU prices * (1+markup)), and use this as the authoritative materials range -- but ALWAYS carry forward default_estimates.materials.note verbatim if present, regardless of which range won.

Invokes the SINGLE unified pricing function for every entry path -- no separate _computePrice. Archaeology finding: the legacy Catalog-browse path's _computePrice never included global_rules.surcharges.dispatch_fee at all, while the SmartQuote path's computeUnifiedQuote always did -- the exact same service could quote differently by the full dispatch fee ($45) depending on which UI entry point was tapped. Confirmed and fixed mid-session by adding the fee to _computePrice directly (a stopgap); this workflow's real fix is that _computePrice no longer exists as a separate function at all once Phase 6 lands -- every entry path reaches this exact step.

ui_template_matrix._note: Determines UI layout. Evaluates top-down, first match wins. Corrected from the roadmap's original example: uses the NAMED flags from compute_variability_flags instead of a single chainIsQtyOnly boolean, specifically so the 3 real bypass_intake services_specified with one genuine non-quantity question (high_ceiling_bulb_replacement, shower_head_replacement, tub_spout_replacement) are NOT routed into a template with no room to ask that question -- confirmed via direct check this session that those exact 3 services_specified have complexity_tier:'skilled', not 'routine', ruling out a naive tier-based rule too.

rules._note: Archaeology Audit finding (confirmed via direct read of prefillSmartQuoteFromService): requires_furniture_selection and the isOther condition (default_tags includes #adhoc or #manual_review) BOTH completely bypass the curated-card/self-quote matrix today, diverting to the legacy showIntakeQuestions flow entirely -- a categorically different UI (a furniture item-picker grid, not a question card). Must be checked FIRST, before any confidence/chain-shape logic, exactly matching the legacy code's own early-return structure. Confirmed via direct data check: only furniture_assembly_flat_pack currently triggers this (requires_furniture_selection); the isOther/#adhoc/#manual_review condition is real, checkable code with zero services_specified currently triggering it -- dormant today, not removed, since a future service could set those tags.

Same legacy_flow bypass as requires_furniture_selection above, triggered instead by default_tags containing #adhoc or #manual_review. Confirmed dormant today (zero real services_specified currently set either tag) but the condition is real, live code in getServiceProfile()/prefillSmartQuoteFromService -- kept as an explicit rule so a future service authored with these tags is correctly routed, not silently mishandled by the matrix's other rules.

Pure quantity-only chain (or empty chain) -- the original, always-correct self-quote case. v9.5: confirmed via a complete catalog sweep that chain_is_pure_quantity's own definition (see orch_compute_variability_flags) correctly encodes the real, full legacy logic -- unconditional qty modules (global_quantity/item_count/etc, no bypass_intake needed) AND item_count_template specifically (which DOES require bypass_intake, checked inside the flag itself, not here) -- so this rule's condition is correctly simple; no separate bypass_intake gate belongs at the matrix level.

Archaeology fix: item_count_template-only services_specified explicitly flagged bypass_intake (cabinet_knob_or_pull_install, door_lock_or_handle_install, led_bulb_upgrade) -- genuinely simple, but NOT chain_is_pure_quantity under the legacy module-name check, which is exactly why they never qualified for self-quote before this session's fix.

v9.5 FIX (the real, final fix, found by a complete catalog sweep -- two earlier attempts at this fix were each incomplete in a different way, see CHANGELOG/BACKLOG for the full story): a genuinely real, valid combination existed with no matching rule -- chain_is_pure_quantity:false (correctly, since the service isn't bypass_intake-eligible) AND chain_has_real_non_quantity_question:false (correctly, since item_count_template alone is never a real, distinguishing question). Confirmed via a complete catalog sweep this affected 10 real services_specified (dimmer_switch_install, gfci_outlet_replacement, etc) -- each has exactly one real question (a qty prompt) that deserves a normal curated_card, not the chip_grid fallback this gap was silently routing them to. Placed after the self_quote rules (which correctly take priority when they DO match) and before the confidence-based catch-all.

Archaeology fix: high_ceiling_bulb_replacement / shower_head_replacement / tub_spout_replacement. MUST NOT route to self_quote -- that template has no room to ask the one real question these services_specified have (mounting_height / existing_type), and forcing them through would silently drop a real, price-affecting answer. force_confidence (90-95 in the real data) applies ONLY once every visible real question is answered -- never before, since these values are deliberately HIGHER than the service's own minimum_quote_confidence and would otherwise let the Estimate button enable before the real question is answered.

Archaeology fix (caught by running the orchestrator against real data, not just reading the matrix on paper): a directly-resolved, real entity with real questions and NO bypass_intake flag was falling through to the confidence_score<60 catch-all (chip_grid/force_dynamic_fallback) -- categorically wrong for e.g. toilet_install, a real named service whose intake chain simply hasn't been answered yet. The confidence_score<60 rule is reserved for the genuinely unresolved/low-confidence free-text case; this rule correctly intercepts the much more common 'real entity, real questions, not yet answered' case first.

intake_bypass_rules._note: Phase 3 (separate from ui_template_matrix's UI-layout decision): determines whether the action/type-selection step (legacy 'Step 2') can be skipped because the answer is already unambiguous. Archaeology finding: the legacy builder (prefillSmartQuoteFromOtherTile/sqOpenBuilderPreseeded) already correctly implements this via uncoveredTypes.length === 1 -- this node makes that real, working logic an explicit, auditable rule rather than inline code only discoverable by reading the builder function.

rules._note: Exactly one possible service_type remains uncovered for this group -- the action is unambiguous, asking the customer to confirm it would be exactly the kind of self-evident question this project has repeatedly found and removed (e.g. asking if a front door is solid-core).

fallback._reason: multiple possible types remain genuinely ambiguous -- must ask

fallback_routing_rules._note: Phase 3: determines where to route when resolve_entity (workflow step 1) cannot pin down a specific named service. Archaeology finding: this exact 3-tier fallback chain (group match -> synthesize Other tile via resolve_dynamic_service; category match only -> generic wizard; no match at all -> intent_mappings.default_fallback) already exists correctly in the legacy sqAnalyze/prefillSmartQuoteFromOtherTile code -- this node makes it an explicit, auditable rule set. Does NOT change behavior; documents and formalizes what the verified-correct legacy code already does.

rules._note: category + group both resolved with reasonable confidence -- the most common real fallback case (e.g. 'mount a projector screen' correctly landing in wall_mounting_tv_flatscreen).

Category known but no specific group match -- ask the customer directly rather than guess a group, exactly the discipline that fixed the original 'groups[0] -> Appliances' misrouting bug found earlier this session.

Genuinely unresolved. Archaeology finding (still open, tracked separately): intent_mappings.default_fallback.service_id references 'generic_handyman_service', which does not exist as a real catalog service, and the field is also never read by any legacy code even when it did resolve. This rule formalizes the INTENDED behavior; the underlying data/content gap (authoring that service, or removing the dangling reference) remains separate, real backlog.

fallback._reason: absolute final safety net -- never silently fail to resolve to SOME bookable path

resolution_thresholds._note: Archaeology Audit finding: these two confidence thresholds were hardcoded in TWO places each (the legacy sqAnalyze AND the Phase 1 collectBookingContext_freeText collector), never read from the SSOT. auto_select_named_service is the bar a free-text match's _matchConfidence must clear to auto-select a specific named service outright (skip straight to its curated card). route_to_group_other_tile is the lower bar for routing to a resolved GROUP's synthetic Other tile when no single named service is confident enough. Both now live here as the single, real source -- orch_resolve_entity and collectBookingContext_freeText both read from this node instead of each carrying their own duplicate literal.

invariants.rule: A ResolvedRoute must be a real, non-null object
invariants._note: The most basic possible guarantee -- if the Orchestrator somehow produces undefined/null, nothing downstream should ever see it.

invariants.rule: uiTemplate must be one of the engine's real, defined templates
invariants._note: Catches a broken or stale ui_template_matrix rule producing a typo'd or removed template name -- exactly the failure mode this was first built to catch (a deliberately broken matrix.fallback during Phase 4 development).

invariants.rule: If uiTemplate is legacy_flow, a real entity must be resolved
invariants._note: legacy_flow specifically hands off to showIntakeQuestions, which requires a real service object -- a furniture-selection or adhoc bypass with no entity has nothing for the legacy renderer to work with.

invariants.rule: self_quote and curated_card require entityType to not be 'fallback'
invariants._note: A question-asking template needs a real, resolved entity to ask questions ABOUT -- entityType:fallback means resolve_entity (workflow step 1) never found one.
A real quote can never bill a negative amount -- catches a sign error or a pricing formula gone wrong upstream.
An inverted range (min > max) is never meaningful and would display nonsensically to a customer.

invariants.rule: trace must be a real, non-empty array
invariants._note: Phase 7 observability (and the Historian replay mechanism) depend on every real route carrying its own execution history -- a route with no trace can never be debugged or replayed.

confidence_escalation._note: Applied live by the pricing engine when a detected/tapped smart_tag carries complexity_override. Worst escalation among all active tags wins — deltas do not stack additively across multiple tags. This lets an unnamed/service_action_types job (e.g. 'Other Wall Mounting: neon sign on brick') tighten its own confidence bar mid-session without needing a hand-authored service for every possible material/condition combo. max_followup_questions_absolute caps total questions regardless of escalation — even a genuinely complex job shouldn't face more than 6 questions, or 'minimal friction' stops meaning anything.

diagnostic_governance._rule: checkout_state == 'diagnostic' IF AND ONLY IF confidence_strategy._variability_tier == 'diagnostic'. variability_tier is the single source of truth for this — checkout_state should be treated as DERIVED from it, not independently authored. This was previously an unenforced coincidence (verified true for all services at time of writing, but nothing prevented a future edit from breaking it).
diagnostic_governance._consuming_code_should: derive checkout_state from variability_tier when both are absent/ambiguous, and treat a stored checkout_state that contradicts variability_tier as a data error to flag, not silently trust.

confidence_strategy._note: v9.5.10 Phase 4 (Confidence Model Reconciliation, from DEVELOPERS_CHECKLIST_ROADMAP_TO_SEESAW.md): raised from 40 -- the checklist's own flagged contradiction was specialized tier sitting at the LOWEST minimum_quote_confidence in the system, across all (then-)16 specialized services_specified, zero exceptions. Confirmed real and substantive before changing anything, not assumed: of this same set, 15 of 16 are checkout_state=standard_flat_rate at baseline (give a real, firm, final price), so this wasn't a case where low confidence was low-stakes because a diagnostic visit would catch any gap anyway. Checked whether skilled tier's own real 40/45/50 spread encoded a duration-based pattern worth extending faithfully (the way T20 extrapolated its 5 real progressive-band services_specified) -- it doesn't: confidence=50 alone spans the entire real 30-90min duration range in skilled tier, no clean signal to extend. This is judgment-based content authoring, not extrapolation, and is labeled as such. hardware_install_formula._note: Real, two-tier model per direct, explicit business input. TIER 1 (the realistic, common case): most requests are a style swap of a handful of pulls/knobs (1-10ish units) -- a single, fair flat_tier_price ($140, ~2hrs @ $70/hr) covers the real, non-trivial setup this job actually involves (often removing the drawer/cabinet door to access hardware from behind), correctly NOT charged per-unit at this scale, since real customers should never feel like they're being billed $2-7 a pull for what is genuinely one job. TIER 2 (genuine bulk, the rare commercial/new-business case): once real per-unit math (base_minutes + rate*qty, at $70/hr) exceeds flat_tier_price, it takes over -- continuous, never a downward cliff, since the real final price is always max(flat_tier_price, per-unit calculation). swap (4 min/unit) and new_holes (5 min/unit) stay genuinely DISTINCT even at bulk volume, by explicit, deliberate decision -- the riskier, more error-prone work (measuring/drilling/marking 75+ times in a row, real fatigue and mis-drill risk) must price higher than a repetitive swap, even though this exact scenario is rare; consistency under an implausible-but-not-impossible case matters more than a clean single number. $70/hr matches the real, already-established rate for general, ad-hoc minor-home-repair labor (see furniture_repair_formula's own override of the same value).

item_count_overflow_formula._note: v9.6 NEW, PLACEHOLDER-DATA FORMULA. Direct request: fix the 'banded quantity price cliff' (T20) where an open-ended top band like '9 or more items' charges the exact same price whether the customer needs 9 or 400. Design: DORMANT by default -- if no explicit exact count is supplied (qty_value on the chosen answer), returns the existing flat top-band price with ZERO behavior change from today. Only activates if a future UI adds a real numeric-count capture for the open-ended band (the same 'specify exact count' pattern already established by pax_wardrobe_formula), at which point it charges the top band's own flat price PLUS a continuous per-unit rate for every unit beyond the band's anchor count -- guaranteed via max() to never charge less than today's flat price. Every per-service overflow_rate_ref below is either extrapolated directly from that service's own authored band progression, or -- where a service has zero fee signal of its own (all bands currently $0) -- borrowed analogically from the most comparable real service, with the specific reasoning recorded on each modifier entry in global_rules.modifiers. ALL flagged for real business-owner confirmation before being treated as final; none are actual customer quotes yet since the mechanism is dormant without new UI.
